review.mr_type_evidence: git log main..HEAD --format=%s → 7x feat, 2x fix, 1x test, 1x docs, 1x style. Feature and fix signals disagree → mixed (diff-heuristics.md § Scoping rules). Both fix commits (f92ece3, 38f1759) repair defects introduced earlier on this same branch and ship tests in the same diff, so MR-COV-07 does not fire.
review.scope: Go only. The 13 web/ TypeScript files and docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-16-medal-product-type-design.md were not reviewed by this skill.

findings.message: UpdateProductWithCourses received the new weight/image preserve branch (store.go:236, store.go:239), but no test exercises the real method. The only tests are shim re-implementations: backend/internal/service/store_test.go:1177 and :1251 declare their own UpdateProductWithCourses on shim structs, and store_test.go:1327 calls svc.UpdateProductWithCourses where svc is &shimUpdateProductWithCoursesAtomic{} (store_test.go:1325) — never the real *Service. Worse, the shim body at store_test.go:1193-1194 still hardcodes the OLD pre-fix logic (p.WeightGrams = existing.WeightGrams; p.ImageURL = existing.ImageURL, unconditional overwrite) — the exact bug commit f92ece3 fixed in production code. The shim will stay green forever regardless of what the real branch does. This is the shim-copy anti-pattern already logged as review debt from PR #25 and PR #32; this MR did not introduce it, but it is the change that makes it actively wrong.
findings.suggestion: Add a real-service test in backend/integration/update_preserve_test.go calling env.svc.UpdateProductWithCourses for the persist-new and preserve-on-omit cases. Separately, realign or delete the stale shim at store_test.go:1181-1194 so it no longer encodes the reverted behavior.
findings.verified: grep -rn 'UpdateProductWithCourses' --include='*_test.go' and sed -n '1175,1200p' internal/service/store_test.go — shim body confirmed to contain the reverted lines.

findings.message: UpdateProductWithExams received the new weight/image preserve branch (store.go:181 `if !p.WeightGramsSet && p.WeightGrams == 0`, store.go:184 `if !p.ImageURLSet && p.ImageURL == ""`), but UpdateProductWithExams has ZERO references in any _test.go across the module. This is one of three copies of the same rule; the sibling copy in UpdateProduct is covered by backend/integration/update_preserve_test.go:87, so the two branches here are protected by nothing. AdminUpdateProduct routes to this path whenever exam_ids is non-nil (backend/internal/handler/product.go:196), so it is live in production.
findings.suggestion: Add a real-service case alongside TestUpdateProduct_PersistsNewWeightImage_ThenPreservesOnOmit_RealService in backend/integration/update_preserve_test.go that calls env.svc.UpdateProductWithExams with (a) a new WeightGrams+ImageURL asserting both persist, and (b) a follow-up call omitting both asserting the last-persisted values survive.

findings.message: TestAdminCompleteOrder_ProcessingMerchandise_RejectedUntilShipped only tests the rejection half. The name promises 'until shipped' but the test never drives the order to 'shipped' and asserts completion then SUCCEEDS. A SUT that unconditionally rejects AdminCompleteOrder for every order — physical or digital — passes this test. The ship-before-complete gate at store.go:989 is guarded in one direction only, so the new isPhysicalType() widening feeding it could be inverted (e.g. !isPhysicalType) and this test would still be green.
findings.suggestion: Add a second subtest: after SetOrderStatus(order, "shipped"), assert require.NoError(t, svc.AdminCompleteOrder(ctx, order.ID.String())) and that the order status becomes 'completed'. Optionally add a digital-only (course/exam) order row asserting it completes from 'processing' without shipping, to pin that the gate is type-scoped rather than blanket.
findings.verified: sed -n '84,110p' internal/service/store_merch_test.go — test body ends at :107 with no shipped-path assertion.

findings.message: The ImageURLSet presence flag introduces a new branch — explicit `image_url: ""` (flag true, value empty) skips the preserve at store.go:294 and clears the stored image, a capability the old unconditional `p.ImageURL = existing.ImageURL` could not express. No test drives it: grep -rn 'ImageURLSet\|WeightGramsSet' --include='*_test.go' returns zero hits module-wide, and no test sends an empty image_url. The symmetric weight case IS covered (backend/internal/handler/product_merch_handler_test.go:172 asserts explicit weight_grams:0 persists), which makes the image omission conspicuous — the two flags exist for exactly the same reason. backend/integration/update_preserve_test.go:87 only exercises the `p.ImageURL == ""` fallback clause because it never sets the flags, so the true-flag path is unreached from either layer.
findings.suggestion: Add a handler-level case mirroring TestAdminUpdateProduct_WeightGramsZero_PersistsAndPreservesOmittedImage: PATCH with `"image_url": ""` on a product that has a stored image, assert the response and the persisted row both show an empty image_url (clearing works), and assert weight_grams is preserved. If clearing the image is NOT intended, the branch should reject empty instead — either way it needs a test to pin the decision.

findings.message: Sentinel-string error assertion: strings.Contains(err.Error(), "must be shipped before completing"). Per anti-patterns.md (golang/08 §10) errors must be matched with errors.Is/As on a typed sentinel. The SUT builds this error inline via errors.New at store.go:989 with no exported sentinel, so the test is coupled to prose that any reword — or an i18n pass — silently breaks. Note the sibling tests in this same file correctly use errors.Is/ErrorIs (lines 24, 32, 68, 81) against ErrForbidden/ErrOutOfStock/ErrNotFound, so this is an inconsistency within the new code, not a repo-wide constraint.
findings.suggestion: Add `ErrMustShipBeforeComplete = errors.New("order has physical items — must be shipped before completing")` to the sentinel block in store.go (already touched by this MR, lines 14-26), return it at store.go:989, and assert require.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrMustShipBeforeComplete).

findings.message: Swallowed fixture errors. Line 159 discards the SignAccess error with `_`, and line 160 calls rdb.Set(...) without checking .Err(). If either fails, the token is empty/unregistered and the request 401s — the test then fails with 'want 403, got 401', pointing the reader at RBAC when the real fault is fixture setup. The mintProductToken helper at line 113 in this same file does check both; this test bypasses it for no stated reason.
findings.suggestion: Reuse the existing helper shape: `tokenString, jti, err := env.signer.SignAccess(...)` with `if err != nil { t.Fatalf("SignAccess: %v", err) }`, and `if err := rdb.Set(...).Err(); err != nil { t.Fatalf("redis set session: %v", err) }`.

severity_deviation.rationale: review-rubric.md buckets swallowed errors as Blocker. Rated request-changes because the governing meta-heuristic ('would a broken implementation slip past this test?') answers no — these are fixture calls, not SUT calls, so a failure still reddens the test, merely with a misleading diagnostic. Flagged for human override; promote to Blocker if the team reads that rule strictly.

findings.message: t.Fatalf is called inside adminProductDBOnce.Do (lines 53, 57, 60, 64, 70). t.Fatalf triggers runtime.Goexit on the first test's goroutine, which still marks the Once as done while leaving adminProductDBEnv nil — every later test then reports the generic 'admin product test env failed to initialize' (line 108) instead of the real container error. Under -shuffle=on, which test carries the real diagnostic varies run to run. Not flagged higher: the container is properly terminated in TestMain (main_test.go:86) and this mirrors the established realdb_test.go:30 realDBOnce convention.
findings.suggestion: Capture the setup error in a package-level var inside the Do closure and surface it after: `if adminProductDBErr != nil { t.Fatalf("admin product env: %v", adminProductDBErr) }`, so every test reports the true cause.

findings.message: TestCheckoutOrder_MerchandiseStockEnforcedAndDecremented drives two distinct behaviors — sufficient-stock decrement (lines 21-27) and insufficient-stock rejection with stock unchanged (lines 30-37) — in one test body with no subtests. A failure in the first half aborts before the second is ever exercised. Kept at nit because every require carries an explanatory message, so this is not true assertion roulette.
findings.suggestion: Split into two t.Run subtests ('sufficient stock decrements', 'insufficient stock rejects and preserves stock') so both branches report independently.

findings.message: time.Now().String() is introduced on an added line for the OutboxEvent.CreatedAt fixture field. Flagged for the record under MR-QUAL-04 (added non-determinism), but this is not a flake source: CreatedAt is never asserted on and pollOutbox branches nothing on it. Same line's `payload, _ := json.Marshal(...)` (line 35) discards an error that cannot fire for this struct, and would fail the test loudly via an empty statusUpdate if it did.
findings.suggestion: Use a frozen literal for clarity — e.g. a package-level `var testCreatedAt = time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC).String()` — so the fixture reads as deliberately fixed rather than incidentally time-dependent.

passed_checks.note: Migration 0027 is covered by backend/internal/repository/migration_0027_test.go against a REAL Postgres (testcontainers postgres:16-alpine via newMigration0025Pool, migration_0025_test.go:25): pre-migration rejection (line 18), post-up acceptance for both merchandise AND medal, a 'bogus' negative control proving the widened CHECK still constrains (line 36 — this is what stops the migration being written as a CHECK drop), fail-safe down converting both types to 'book' rather than orphaning, and post-down re-rejection. No sqlmock and no SQLite anywhere in the module, so the anti-patterns.md hard-blocks do not fire. This is the strongest part of the MR.

Both fix commits (f92ece3, 38f1759) ship test files in the same diff, failing detection step 3. f92ece3's regression is genuinely pinned by backend/integration/update_preserve_test.go:87, which would fail on the parent commit.

None found among the NEW test files. worker/outbox_merch_test.go:71-72 builds &Worker{repo: repo} and calls the real w.pollOutbox; mockRepository is a pre-existing hand-rolled fake satisfying the real outboxRepository interface (outbox.go:50) at the DB boundary — the correct double per the fakes-over-mocks decision tree, not a re-declared copy of SUT logic. handler/product_merch_handler_test.go routes through real echo handlers + real service + real testcontainers Postgres. The stale shim in R-01 is pre-existing, not introduced here.

No expected value is derived from the SUT's physical-type list. Every expectation is a hand-written literal ('processing', 'Merchandise', 'Medal', 'book', stock 3/1, weight 350/0). outbox_merch_test.go:19 iterates a literal []string{"merchandise", "medal"} declared in the test, not read from isPhysicalType.

The medal tests are NOT copy-paste duplicates of the merchandise ones and were explicitly not flagged. repository/order.go:404 spells the guard as three separate literals and worker/outbox.go:227 as `case "book", "merchandise", "medal"` — deleting the medal literal from either is caught only by the medal test. Same for buildPaymentRequest's per-type Category arms (store.go:600-607).

The two tests are NOT contradictory: handler/product_merch_handler_test.go:172 sends weight_grams:0 as an explicit JSON value (*int non-nil → WeightGramsSet=true) so 0 persists, while integration/update_preserve_test.go's second update omits the field at the service layer (Set=false, zero value) so 350 is preserved. Both match `if !p.WeightGramsSet && p.WeightGrams == 0` at store.go:181. A genuinely valuable pair.

not_reviewed: MR-COV-01 — no new non-test .go files in the diff; every added file is _test.go, .sql, or .md.

MR-COV-03 — no new exported funcs/types/vars/consts. The new exported FIELDS model.Product.WeightGramsSet/ImageURLSet (backend/internal/model/product.go:14-15) fall outside this scenario's detection regex; their gap is reported under R-04 rather than double-counted.
MR-COV-04 — no new packages/directories in the diff.
MR-QUAL-05 — no test-only package changes; every touched test package also has production changes.
The 13 web/ TypeScript files and their Vitest tests — this skill is Go-scoped. Covered separately by the accompanying line-by-line review, whose findings are NOT in this JSON.

informational.note: This repo has ZERO //go:build integration tags module-wide — testcontainers tests run inside the default go test suite by design. MR-COV-05's literal build-tag requirement is met in substance (real Postgres) but not in form. Pre-existing repo-wide convention, not introduced by this MR. Not a finding.

The physical-type list is now duplicated across three packages: service.isPhysicalType (store.go:1199), repository/order.go:404, and worker/outbox.go:227, each with its own inline literals; order.go:399 documents this as deliberate to avoid a repo→service import. No test pins the three lists in agreement, so a future fourth physical type added to one but not the others would ship silently. Not a finding against the tests in this diff; raised because it is the structural risk the merch/medal tests are compensating for.

next_steps: Verdict is request-changes: address the six Request-changes findings; Nits at author's discretion.
R-01 and R-03 are the two worth doing before merge: R-01 because a green test currently asserts reverted behavior, R-03 because the feature's core gate is half-tested.
When addressing R-01/R-02, the followup is /ralp-test-plan on internal/service — review-test does not write tests.
This review has not been posted anywhere. Publishing to a PR is a separate explicit step; this skill never approves.
